strategic_profile.executive_summary: The University of Tennessee, Knoxville is a large, public land-grant research university and the flagship campus of the state. Fueled by a massive surge in out-of-state applications and strong SEC appeal, it has rapidly transitioned into a highly selective institution requiring standardized test scores.
strategic_profile.market_position: State Flagship / Major Public Research University

analyst_takeaways.insight: Acceptance rates dropped from ~75% in 2021 to 41.6% in 2024 due to an influx of nearly 60,000 applications.
analyst_takeaways.implication: UTK is no longer a safety school for out-of-state applicants; it is highly competitive and requires strong academic and testing profiles.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Median earnings 10 years post-enrollment are $67,631, with a median debt of $20,500.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Provides a strong ROI, particularly for in-state students who qualify for the Tri-Star or Volunteer scholarship programs.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Surging enrollment has led to capacity issues, notably in housing and campus parking.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Prospective students should be prepared for logistical hurdles and the necessity of utilizing alternative campus transportation.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Highly spirited, traditional SEC campus life dominated by Division I athletics, Greek life, and over 450 student organizations.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Parking is highly limited; students rely heavily on the fare-free 'T' bus system, KAT buses, and the UT to West TN shuttle for academic breaks.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: R1 university with $324.4M in research expenditures and strategic partnerships with Oak Ridge National Laboratory.

current_status.test_policy_details: ACT or SAT scores are required for all first-year applicants for admission and institutional merit scholarships. Superscoring is utilized.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist utilization spiked massively in 2024 to manage yield amidst application inflation.

testing.policy_note: Test required. Historically, 81% submitted ACT and 24% submitted SAT prior to the mandate.

colleges.name: Haslam College of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students seeking highly-ranked logistics and corporate business pathways.

majors.name: Supply Chain Management
majors.notes: Consistently ranked among the top 3 supply chain undergraduate programs nationally.

colleges.name: Tickle College of Engineering
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Best for high-stats STEM applicants who meet the minimum SPI of 60 and 25 Math ACT.

majors.name: Mechanical Engineering
majors.special_requirements: 3.4 GPA required for BS/MS accelerated pathway or Honors.
majors.notes: Students without direct admission can earn provisional status but must maintain at least a 2.0 GPA in early core courses.

colleges.name: College of Arts & Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students pursuing humanities, liberal arts, or non-impacted sciences.

majors.name: Psychology
majors.notes: Students must maintain a 2.0 GPA to remain in the major.

application_deadlines.notes: Critical for maximum institutional merit scholarship consideration.

supplemental_requirements.details: One required essay chosen from seven prompts. Optional supporting statement allowed.
Mandatory Self-Reported Transcript and Academic Record System.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: UTK heavily prioritizes in-state top 10% students due to state mandate.

major_selection_tactics: Select an alternative major if applying to capped programs like Nursing, Engineering, or Architecture.
Utilize 'University Exploratory' if truly undecided to enter without a specific college restriction.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: UTK fully considers applicants for their alternative major. Students placed in the 'University Transition' program receive accelerated advising to declare a new major by their second full semester.

scholarships.name: Tri-Star Scholarship Program
scholarships.benefits: Covers tuition and mandatory fees when combined with state/federal aid.

scholarships.name: Distinguished Tennessean Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Awarded to one student from every TN county.

scholarships.name: Orange & White Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Base merit for 3.6 GPA and 24 ACT.

ap_policy.general_rule: Awards credit for most AP scores of 4 or 5.
exceptions: A score of 3 on AP English Language may grant placement into ENGL 112 if paired with a 29+ ACT or 680+ SAT.
ap_policy.usage: Credits can fulfill general education requirements or act as prerequisites.

ib_policy.general_rule: Accepts higher level exams with passing scores.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Transfer applicants with fewer than 30 hours must have a 2.5 college GPA and meet freshmen standards.

what_it_takes: A core GPA approaching 4.0 or top 10% class rank for in-state students.
An ACT of 25+ as a baseline, higher for OOS or competitive majors.
common_activities: Research with Oak Ridge National Lab
essay_tips: Share personal experiences indicating potential for success.
Focus on leadership and community building.
Do not invent a persona; write authentically.
red_flags: Failing to accurately complete the STARS self-reported transcript.
Submitting an application for Engineering/Nursing without competitive test scores.
